title: Introduction to move2Acc
text: create acceleration vector list acceleration measurements combined measurement frequency. element list matrix columns axis acceleration measured rows repeated measurements. printed reports average acceleration axis burst measurement frequency. Multiple bursts can combined diverse properties can combined.
code: x <- acc(   bursts = list(cbind(     x = sin(1:30 / 10),     y = cos(1:30 / 10),     z = 1:30 / 10   )),   frequency = units::as_units(40, "Hz") ) x y <- acc(   bursts = list(     cbind(x = sin(1:20 / 10), y = cos(1:20 / 10)),     cbind(x = sin(1:20 / 10 + 2), y = cos(1:20 / 10 + 3))   ),   frequency = units::as_units(30, "Hz") ) y c(x, y)

title: Introduction to move2Acc
text: Naturally time data won’t generated directly R rather loaded local files databases like movebank. data movebank quick conversion options build. take move2 objects input. now conversion functions data eobs ornitella tags exists. example eobs tags. as_acc function applied move2 attempts detect columns representing acceleration data. can done ornitella data.
code: require(dplyr, quietly = TRUE) require(move2, quietly = TRUE) kinkajous_data <- movebank_download_study("Kinkajous on Pipeline Road Panama",   sensor_type_id = c("acceleration", "gps"), "license-md5" = "6e1743b4b2a919df0f3b4167c94786d9" ) kinkajous_data <- kinkajous_data %>%   mutate(acceleration = as_acc(.)) %>%   select(acceleration) tail(kinkajous_data) d <- as.Date("2021-3-3")  lbbg_data <- movebank_download_study("LBBG_ZEEBRUGGE - Lesser black-backed gulls ",   sensor_type_id = c("gps", "acceleration"),   timestamp_start = as.POSIXct(d),   timestamp_end = as.POSIXct(d + 1) ) lbbg_data <- lbbg_data %>%   mutate(acceleration = as_acc(.)) %>%   filter(!is.na(acceleration) | sensor_type_id==653) %>%   select(acceleration) lbbg_data mt_stack(lbbg_data, kinkajous_data)

title: Introduction to move2Acc
text: plot_time function possible explor acceleration bursts. plot can zoom using mouse. Alternatively can plot one burst directly, case showing clear wing beats z axis. can also visualize one burst three dimensions showing cyclic patterns acceleration.
code: plot_time(lbbg_data$acceleration, mt_time(lbbg_data)) plot_time(lbbg_data$acceleration[340], mt_time(lbbg_data)[340]) bb<-vctrs::field(lbbg_data$acceleration[340],"bursts")[[1]] b<-units::drop_units(bb) e<-0.1 persp(z=matrix(min(b[,'tilt_z'])-e, ncol=2, nrow=2),       x=range(b[,'tilt_x']),       y=range(b[,'tilt_y']),              xlim=range(b[,'tilt_x'])+c(-e,e),       ylim=range(b[,'tilt_y'])+c(-e,e),        zlim=range(b[,'tilt_z'])+c(-e,e),       border=NA,       xlab=paste0("X [",as.character(units(bb)),']'),        ticktype = "detailed", cex.axis = 0.65,       ylab=paste0("Y [",as.character(units(bb)),']'),        zlab=paste0("Z [",as.character(units(bb)),']'),        theta = -160, phi = 20, expand = 0.5, col = "white", scale=F)->p # Draw line on bottom of plot lines(trans3d(b[,'tilt_x'],b[,'tilt_y'],min(b[,'tilt_z'])-e,p), col="tomato") # Lines to connect observations to bottom apply(b,1, function(x, bottom){   lines(trans3d(x['tilt_x'],x['tilt_y'],c(x['tilt_z'], bottom-e),p), col="gray") }, bottom=min(b[,'tilt_z']))  lines(trans3d(b[,'tilt_x'],b[,'tilt_y'],b[,'tilt_z'],p), col="red") points(trans3d(b[,'tilt_x'],b[,'tilt_y'],b[,'tilt_z'],p), col="red", pch=19) plot_time(lbbg_data$acceleration[334], mt_time(lbbg_data)[340]) bb<-vctrs::field(lbbg_data$acceleration[334],"bursts")[[1]]  z<-bb[,3] m1<-Mod(fft(c(z,rep(units::set_units(0, 'standard_free_fall'), length(z)*199)))) (which.max(tail(head(m1, round(length(m1)/2)),-200))+200)* vctrs::field(lbbg_data$acceleration[340],"frequency") /(nrow(bb)*200)

title: Authors and Citation
text: Bart Kranstauber. Author, maintainer.

title: Authors and Citation
text: Kranstauber B (2026). move2Acc: Handling bursted acceleration data. R package version 0.1.0, https://github.com/bart1/move2Acc.
code: @Manual{,   title = {move2Acc: Handling bursted acceleration data},   author = {Bart Kranstauber},   year = {2026},   note = {R package version 0.1.0},   url = {https://github.com/bart1/move2Acc}, }

title: Handling bursted acceleration data
text: Keep frequency axes per element vector? -> per element changes within study Allow NA’s? -> yes vector , burst? start time part vector? -> probably move2 already keeps Calibrated (G) Uncalibrated -> might covered storing units assume constant sampling freq burst

title: Handling bursted acceleration data
text: can install development version move2Acc like :
code: # install.packages("remotes") remotes::install_github("bart1/move2Acc")

title: Handling bursted acceleration data
text: basic example shows solve common problem:
code: library(move2Acc) ## basic example code

title: Create a acc vector — acc
text: Create acc vector

title: Create a acc vector — acc
code: acc(   bursts = list(),   frequency = units::set_units(double(), "Hz"),   start = NULL )

title: Create a acc vector — acc
text: bursts list matrices frequency sampling frequency recordings bursts. Either length bursts recycled. units specified, frequency assumed Hz. start Start time burst, POSIXct format

what: Convert raw acceleration values to physical units in an acc vector — acc_calibrate
title: Convert raw acceleration values to physical units in an acc vector — acc_calibrate
text: Applies calibration function(s) bursts raw acceleration ADC values acc vector. Specify set calibration functions acc_calibration().

title: Convert raw acceleration values to physical units in an acc vector — acc_calibrate
code: acc_calibrate(x, calibration)

title: Convert raw acceleration values to physical units in an acc vector — acc_calibrate
text: x acc vector. calibration acc_calibration object containing calibration function(s) apply burst x. See acc_calibration() specify calibration functions.

title: Convert raw acceleration values to physical units in an acc vector — acc_calibrate
text: acc vector length input calibrated burst matrices.

title: Convert raw acceleration values to physical units in an acc vector — acc_calibrate
code: a <- acc_example()  acc_calibrate(a, acc_calibration("ornitela")) #> <acceleration[2]> #> [1] (0.01 0 0.01) [m/s^2]  (0 -0.01 0.01) [m/s^2] #> # frequency: 20 [Hz]  acc_calibrate(a, acc_calibration("eobs", tag_id = 1000)) #> <acceleration[2]> #> [1] (-54.21 -54.23 -54.2) [m/s^2] (-54.22 -54.24 -54.2) [m/s^2] #> # frequency: 20 [Hz]  acc_calibrate(a, acc_calibration(offset = 2048, slope = 0.001)) #> <acceleration[2]> #> [1] (-20.08 -20.08 -20.07) [m/s^2] (-20.08 -20.09 -20.07) [m/s^2] #> # frequency: 20 [Hz]  # Specify different calibration parameters for each burst. # Calibrations will be mapped to the input `acc` object by index. acc_calibrate(   a,    acc_calibration(offset = c(2048, 2046), slope = c(0.001, 0.002)) ) #> <acceleration[2]> #> [1] (-20.08 -20.08 -20.07) [m/s^2] (-40.13 -40.14 -40.11) [m/s^2] #> # frequency: 20 [Hz]

what: Create calibration functions for raw acceleration values — acc_calibration
title: Create calibration functions for raw acceleration values — acc_calibration
text: Generate acc_calibration object containing list functions various calibration parameters used acc_calibrate(). Use acc_calibration() specify calibration parameters manually. Arguments vectorized matched index. Use as_acc_calibration() convert data.frame containing row-wise burst calibration parameters acc_calibration object. allows specify burst-specific calibration functions flexibly convert raw acceleration values physical units acc vectors contain data heterogeneous sources.

title: Create calibration functions for raw acceleration values — acc_calibration
code: acc_calibration(   manufacturer = NULL,   tag_id = NULL,   sensitivity = NULL,   offset = NULL,   offset_x = offset,   offset_y = offset,   offset_z = offset,   slope = NULL,   slope_x = slope,   slope_y = slope,   slope_z = slope,   orientation = NULL,   orientation_x = orientation,   orientation_y = orientation,   orientation_z = orientation,   units = "m/s^2",   axes = "XYZ" )  as_acc_calibration(df)

title: Create calibration functions for raw acceleration values — acc_calibration
text: manufacturer Manufacturer tag. Currently, "eobs" "ornitela" supported. manufacturers, leave NULL manually specify calibration parameters . tag_id manufacturer = "eobs", e-obs tag ID tag. sensitivity manufacturer = "eobs", sensitivity tag. Defaults "low" none provided. Note e-obs generation 1 tags sensitivity setting. offset, offset_x, offset_y, offset_z Custom offset use calibrating. specify axis-specific offsets, use offset_x, offset_y, /offset_z. Required manufacturer specified. slope, slope_x, slope_y, slope_z Custom slope use calibrating. specify axis-specific slope, use slope_x, slope_y, /slope_z. Required manufacturer specified. orientation, orientation_x, orientation_y, orientation_z Either 1 -1 indicating orientation tag's axes. specify axis-specific orientations, use orientation_x, orientation_y, /orientation_z. Defaults 1. useful standardize orientations across tags different manufacturers generations. units Output units. Either "m/s^2" (default) "standard_free_fall". axes Character string specifying axes calibrate, e.g., "XYZ" (default), "XY", "Z". axes appear calibrated output. df data.frame containing columns corresponding available arguments acc_calibration()

title: Create calibration functions for raw acceleration values — acc_calibration
text: acc_calibration object.

title: Create calibration functions for raw acceleration values — acc_calibration
code: # Calibration for ornitela tags: acc_calibration(manufacturer = "ornitela") #> <acc_calibration[1]>  # E-obs tag defaults vary by tag_id and sensitivity (default `"low"`) acc_calibration(manufacturer = "eobs", tag_id = 1000, sensitivity = "high") #> <acc_calibration[1]> acc_calibration(manufacturer = "eobs", tag_id = 4000) #> <acc_calibration[1]>  # Provide vector arguments to generate element-wise calibrations: acc_calibration(   manufacturer = c("eobs", "ornitela"),   tag_id = c(1000, NA) ) #> <acc_calibration[2]>  # Calibration with explicit offset and slope acc_calibration(offset = 2048, slope = 1 / 512) #> <acc_calibration[1]>  # Calibrate specific axes with axis-specific args: cal <- acc_calibration(   offset_x = 2048,    offset_y = 2046,   offset_z = 2048,   slope = 1 / 512,    orientation_y = -1 # Flip y axis orientation )  # Apply calibration with acc_calibrate() acc_calibrate(acc_example(), cal) #> <acceleration[2]> #> [1] (-39.21 39.19 -39.21) [m/s^2] (-39.23 39.2 -39.21) [m/s^2]  #> # frequency: 20 [Hz]

title: Example acc vector — acc_example
text: simple acc vector use examples.

title: Example acc vector — acc_example
code: acc_example()

title: Example acc vector — acc_example
text: acc vector two bursts.

what: Identify IMU columns present in a move2 object — active_colsets
title: Identify IMU columns present in a move2 object — active_colsets
text: Determine column sets used default extracting IMU data move2 object. Column sets processed independently, single move2 may contain multiple active column sets one IMU sensor. active_acc_colsets() — column sets used as_acc(). active_mag_colsets() — column sets used as_mag(). active_gyro_colsets() — column sets used as_gyro().

title: Identify IMU columns present in a move2 object — active_colsets
code: active_acc_colsets(x)  active_mag_colsets(x)  active_gyro_colsets(x)

title: Identify IMU columns present in a move2 object — active_colsets
text: x move2 object.

title: Identify IMU columns present in a move2 object — active_colsets
text: list imu_colset objects.

title: Identify IMU columns present in a move2 object — active_colsets
text: active colsets found, use imu_colset() specify columns contain IMU data.

title: Identify IMU columns present in a move2 object — active_colsets
code: active_acc_colsets(albatrosses()) #> $eobs #> burst-format [axes=eobs_acceleration_axes, frequency=eobs_acceleration_sampling_frequency_per_axis, bursts=eobs_accelerations_raw] #>   # Multiple colsets may be available active_acc_colsets(move2::mt_stack(albatrosses(), gulls())) #> $eobs #> burst-format [axes=eobs_acceleration_axes, frequency=eobs_acceleration_sampling_frequency_per_axis, bursts=eobs_accelerations_raw] #>  #> $raw_xyz #> long-format [X=acceleration_raw_x, Y=acceleration_raw_y, Z=acceleration_raw_z] #>   # Missing long-format axes are not included in the set g <- gulls() g$acceleration_raw_x <- NULL active_acc_colsets(g) #> $raw_xyz #> long-format [=acceleration_raw_y, =acceleration_raw_z] #>   # Columns with no data are also removed g$acceleration_raw_y <- NA active_acc_colsets(g) #> $raw_xyz #> long-format [=acceleration_raw_z] #>   # Some column sets must be present in their entirety alb <- albatrosses() alb$eobs_acceleration_axes <- NULL  if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{   active_acc_colsets(alb) } # }

title: Convert to acc — as_acc
text: many cases as_acc function directly create acceleration vector input data

title: Convert to acc — as_acc
code: as_acc(x, ...)  # Default S3 method as_acc(x, ...)  # S3 method for class 'move2' as_acc(   x,   colset = NULL,   min_freq = 1,   merge_continuous = TRUE,   drop = FALSE,   ... )

title: Convert to acc — as_acc
text: x move2 containing acceleration data collected EOBS, Ornitela, similar tracking devices. time either loaded disk using move2::mt_read downloaded using move2::movebank_download_study. ... currently used colset imu_colset object list imu_colset objects specifying columns x contain acceleration data. default, constructs bursts column sets detected x also contain data (see active_acc_colsets()). Several common colsets listed movebank_acc_colsets(). specify custom set columns, use imu_colset(). min_freq Numeric value indicating minimum allowable within-burst data collection frequency identifying bursts long-format data. two adjacent timestamps fall outside period defined frequency split separate bursts. units provided, value assumed Hz. Ignored data already predefined bursts. merge_continuous Logical value indicating whether merge adjacent bursts. Two adjacent bursts can merged first burst ends time second starts burst frequency identical two. useful processing continuous data stored chunks split regular intervals. drop Logical indicating whether empty bursts dropped output. drop = FALSE, length output match number rows input data x bursts stored index location corresponding start time burst.

title: Convert to acc — as_acc
text: resulting vector long input. means can, example, added column data.frame. tags means NA values inserted one burst stored multiple rows data.frame.

Two adjacent bursts can merged first burst ends time second starts burst frequency identical two. useful processing continuous data stored chunks split regular intervals. drop Logical indicating whether empty bursts dropped output. drop = FALSE, length output match number rows input data x bursts stored index location corresponding start time burst.

what: Calculate dynamic body acceleration (DBA) for an acc vector — vedba
title: Calculate dynamic body acceleration (DBA) for an acc vector — vedba
text: Compute vectorial dynamic body acceleration (VeDBA) overall dynamic body acceleration (ODBA) burst acc vector. Dynamic body acceleration computed subtracting static acceleration component axis summarizing remaining dynamic acceleration : VeDBA: mean Euclidean norm across samples ODBA: mean sum absolute values across samples

what: Identify rows of a move2 object with duplicated IMU data — duplicated_rows
title: Identify rows of a move2 object with duplicated IMU data — duplicated_rows
text: Return row indices move2 object one column set given sensor contains data. Functions extract IMU data error single timestamp contains multiple sources IMU data sensor. resolve duplicated rows, pass specific set IMU columns colset argument as_*() remove duplicated data. duplicated_acc_rows() — checks acceleration column sets used as_acc(). duplicated_mag_rows() — checks magnetometer column sets used as_mag(). duplicated_gyro_rows() — checks gyroscope column sets used as_gyro().

title: Identify rows of a move2 object with duplicated IMU data — duplicated_rows
code: duplicated_acc_rows(x, colsets = NULL)  duplicated_mag_rows(x, colsets = NULL)  duplicated_gyro_rows(x, colsets = NULL)

title: Identify rows of a move2 object with duplicated IMU data — duplicated_rows
text: integer vector row indices duplicated data across column sets.

title: move2Acc example datasets — example_data
text: move2Acc provides two example datasets working acceleration data. data publicly available downloaded Movebank. See Movebank Attribute Dictionary details data attributes.

title: move2Acc example datasets — example_data
text: GPS acceleration data Galapagos albatrosses Movebank study 2911040. Waved albatrosses tracked breeding non-breeding periods 2008 2010. Acceleration data provided burst format e-obs tags. Format: move2 9 tracks 54 features lasting 2008-07-27 00:00:00 UTC 2008-07-27 01:00:00 UTC. Source: https://www.movebank.org/cms/webapp?gwt_fragment=page=studies,path=study2911040

title: move2Acc example datasets — example_data
text: GPS acceleration data lesser black-backed gulls (Larus fuscus, Laridae) breeding southern North Sea coast Belgium Netherlands. Published Research Institute Nature Forest (INBO). Data collected LifeWatch GPS tracking network large birds project/study LBBG_ZEEBRUGGE. Acceleration data provided long format trackers developed University Amsterdam Bird Tracking System (UvA-BiTS). Format: move2 5 tracks 1499 features lasting 2021-03-03 00:00:00 UTC 2021-03-04 00:00:00 UTC. Source: https://www.movebank.org/cms/webapp?gwt_fragment=page=studies,path=study985143423

what: Functions to explore an IMU vector — is_acc
title: Functions to explore an IMU vector — is_acc
text: Functions explore IMU vector

title: Functions to explore an IMU vector — is_acc
code: is_acc(x)  n_axis(x)  is_uniform(x)  bursts(x)  bursts(x) <- value  freqs(x)  freqs(x) <- value  starts(x)  starts(x) <- value  burst_dur(x)  n_samples(x)  imu_units(x)  is_gyro(x)  is_mag(x)

title: Functions to explore an IMU vector — is_acc
text: x IMU vector (e.g. acc, mag, gyro). value Replacement value.

title: Functions to explore an IMU vector — is_acc
code: x <- acc(   bursts = list(     cbind(X = sin(1:30 / 10), Y = cos(1:30 / 10), Z = 1),     cbind(X = sin(1:20 / 10 + 2), Y = cos(1:20 / 10 + 3))   ),   frequency = units::as_units(c(20, 30), "Hz") ) x <- c(x, NA) n_axis(x) #> [1]  3  2 NA n_samples(x) #> [1] 30 20 NA is_uniform(x) #> [1] FALSE length(x) #> [1] 3 is.na(x) #> [1] FALSE FALSE  TRUE na.omit(x) #> <acceleration[2]> #> [1] (0.67 0.01 1) (0.08 -0.52)  #> # frequency: 20 [Hz] - 30 [Hz]  y <- acc(   bursts = list(     cbind(X = sin(1:20 / 10), Y = cos(1:20 / 10)),     cbind(X = sin(1:20 / 10 + 2), Y = cos(1:20 / 10 + 3))   ),   frequency = units::as_units(c(20, 20), "Hz") ) is_uniform(y) #> [1] TRUE

text: bursts list matrices frequency frequency gyroscope recordings. Either length bursts recycled. units specified, frequency assumed Hz. start Start time burst, POSIXct format

what: Specify IMU data columns present in a move2 object — imu_colset
title: Specify IMU data columns present in a move2 object — imu_colset
text: Define columns move2 object contain IMU data. Pass result colset argument as_acc(), as_mag(), as_gyro() convert columns IMU vector. move2 objects store IMU data two ways: Long-format columns store one measurement (possibly multiple axes) single row. Burst-format columns store burst measurements space-delimited string. string must segmented axis-specific measurements using associated column indicates axes present bursted data. column provides sampling frequency burst. three columns must present form valid burst-format column set.

title: Specify IMU data columns present in a move2 object — imu_colset
code: imu_colset(   x = NULL,   y = NULL,   z = NULL,   bursts = NULL,   axes = NULL,   frequency = NULL )

title: Specify IMU data columns present in a move2 object — imu_colset
text: x, y, z (Long-format) Column name(s) X, Y, /Z axes. bursts (Burst-format) Column name containing raw burst strings. axes (Burst-format) Column name containing axis labels burst. frequency (Burst-format) Column name containing sampling frequency burst.

title: Specify IMU data columns present in a move2 object — imu_colset
text: imu_colset object type "long" "burst".

title: Specify IMU data columns present in a move2 object — imu_colset
Create a mag vector — mag

title: Create a mag vector — mag
code: mag(   bursts = list(),   frequency = units::set_units(double(), "Hz"),   start = NULL )

what: Apply a function to each element of an IMU vector — map_imu
title: Apply a function to each element of an IMU vector — map_imu
text: function provides general framework apply arbitrary function element IMU vector providing access element's burst, frequency, start time metadata. Note common operations already implemented stand-alone functions.

title: Apply a function to each element of an IMU vector — map_imu
code: map_imu(x, .f, simplify = FALSE, .progress = FALSE)

title: Apply a function to each element of an IMU vector — map_imu
text: x IMU vector (e.g. acc, mag, gyro). .f function applied element x. can supplied one following ways: named function anonymous function (e.g., function(.br) nrow(.br) / .fq) formula (e.g., ~ nrow(.br) / .fq) cases, use .br refer burst matrix element, .fq refer frequency element, .st refer start time element. See examples. simplify Logical. TRUE, attempts simplify output vector. Otherwise, output list. output simplified maintaining one--one correspondence input, error thrown. .progress Whether show progress bar. Use TRUE turn basic progress bar, use string give name.

title: Apply a function to each element of an IMU vector — map_imu
text: function behaves similarly purrr::map() family functions. However, map_imu() matches arguments name, position. Thus, input .f must use specified terminology (.br, .fq, /.st) access specific data element. given vector x: .br accesses element list returned bursts() .fq accesses element vector returned freqs() .st accesses element vector returned starts()

title: Apply a function to each element of an IMU vector — map_imu
code: a <- acc_example()  # Use `.br` to access the burst matrix for each element: n_samp <- map_imu(a, function(.br) nrow(.br))  n_samp #> [[1]] #> [1] 30 #>  #> [[2]] #> [1] 20 #>   # Use `.fq` to access the frequency value for each element: burst_len <- map_imu(a, function(.br, .fq) nrow(.br) / .fq)  burst_len #> [[1]] #> 1.5 [1/Hz] #>  #> [[2]] #> 1 [1/Hz] #>   # Use `.st` to access the start time for each element: burst_end <- map_imu(   a,   function(.br, .fq, .st) as.numeric(nrow(.br) / .fq) + .st )  burst_end #> [[1]] #> [1] "1970-01-01 00:00:01 UTC" #>  #> [[2]] #> [1] "1970-01-01 00:00:11 UTC" #>   # You can also provide a separately defined function get_burst_end <- function(.br, .fq, .st, offset = 2) {   as.numeric(nrow(.br) / .fq) + .st + offset }  map_imu(a, get_burst_end) #> [[1]] #> [1] "1970-01-01 00:00:03 UTC" #>  #> [[2]] #> [1] "1970-01-01 00:00:13 UTC" #>   map_imu(a, function(.br, .fq, .st) get_burst_end(.br, .fq, .st, offset = 5)) #> [[1]] #> [1] "1970-01-01 00:00:06 UTC" #>  #> [[2]] #> [1] "1970-01-01 00:00:16 UTC" #>   # Use simplify to reduce to a vector format: map_imu(a, get_burst_end, simplify = TRUE) #> [1] "1970-01-01 00:00:03 UTC" "1970-01-01 00:00:13 UTC"  # Note that this will fail if the result cannot be simplified to the same # length as the input vector try(   map_imu(a, function(.br) .br, simplify = TRUE) ) #> Error in purrr::list_simplify(out) :  #>   `x[[1]]` must have size 1, not size 30.

what: Merge adjacent bursts in an IMU vector — merge_imu
title: Merge adjacent bursts in an IMU vector — merge_imu
text: given IMU vector, identify temporally adjacent bursts merge single burst. Bursts end time start time next burst considered adjacent. Bursts different frequencies axes merged.

title: Merge adjacent bursts in an IMU vector — merge_imu
code: merge_imu(x, ids = NULL, drop = FALSE)

title: Merge adjacent bursts in an IMU vector — merge_imu
text: x IMU vector (e.g. acc, mag, gyro). ids Vector indicating groups elements x belong. provided, bursts x merged across different values vector, even timestamps frequencies align. drop Logical indicating whether drop entries merged bursts. drop = FALSE (default), output length input x, NA values positions bursts merged preceding burst. useful retaining index matching input output vectors.

title: Merge adjacent bursts in an IMU vector — merge_imu
text: vector class x.

title: Merge adjacent bursts in an IMU vector — merge_imu
code: a <- acc(   list(cbind(X = 1:60, Y = 1:60), cbind(X = 61:100, Y = 61:100), cbind(X = 101:140)),   frequency = units::set_units(20, "Hz"),   start = as.POSIXct(c(0, 3, 5), tz = "UTC") )  merge_imu(a) #> <acceleration[3]> #> [1] (50.5 50.5) <NA>        (120.5)     #> # frequency: 20 [Hz]

title: View standard Movebank IMU data column sets — movebank_colsets
code: movebank_acc_colsets()  movebank_mag_colsets()  movebank_gyro_colsets()

title: View standard Movebank IMU data column sets — movebank_colsets
text: named list imu_colset objects.

title: View standard Movebank IMU data column sets — movebank_colsets
text: move2 objects store IMU data two ways: Long-format columns store one measurement (possibly multiple axes) single row. Burst-format columns store burst measurements space-delimited string. string must segmented axis-specific measurements using associated column indicates axes present bursted data. column provides sampling frequency burst. three columns must present form valid burst-format column set.

title: View standard Movebank IMU data column sets — movebank_colsets
code: movebank_acc_colsets() #> $eobs #> burst-format [axes=eobs_acceleration_axes, frequency=eobs_acceleration_sampling_frequency_per_axis, bursts=eobs_accelerations_raw] #>  #> $burst #> burst-format [axes=acceleration_axes, frequency=acceleration_sampling_frequency_per_axis, bursts=accelerations_raw] #>  #> $xyz #> long-format [X=acceleration_x, Y=acceleration_y, Z=acceleration_z] #>  #> $raw_xyz #> long-format [X=acceleration_raw_x, Y=acceleration_raw_y, Z=acceleration_raw_z] #>  movebank_mag_colsets() #> $burst #> burst-format [bursts=magnetic_fields_raw, axes=magnetic_field_axes, frequency=magnetic_field_sampling_frequency_per_axis] #>  #> $xyz #> long-format [X=magnetic_field_x, Y=magnetic_field_y, Z=magnetic_field_z] #>  #> $raw_xyz #> long-format [X=magnetic_field_raw_x, Y=magnetic_field_raw_y, Z=magnetic_field_raw_z] #>  movebank_gyro_colsets() #> $burst #> burst-format [bursts=angular_velocities_raw, axes=gyroscope_axes, frequency=gyroscope_sampling_frequency_per_axis] #>  #> $xyz #> long-format [X=angular_velocity_x, Y=angular_velocity_y, Z=angular_velocity_z] #>

what: Calculate the peak frequency per axis for bursts — peak_frequency
title: Calculate the peak frequency per axis for bursts — peak_frequency
text: Calculate peak frequency per axis bursts

title: Calculate the peak frequency per axis for bursts — peak_frequency
code: peak_frequency(x, resolution = NA)

title: Calculate the peak frequency per axis for bursts — peak_frequency
text: x IMU vector (e.g. acc, mag, gyro). resolution scalar units Hertz

title: Calculate the peak frequency per axis for bursts — peak_frequency
text: returns list length x peak frequency per axis

title: Calculate the peak frequency per axis for bursts — peak_frequency
text: increase resolution result zero padding can used. can controlled using resolution argument. Note increasing resolution without increasing number samples burst limited ability get closer true frequency.

title: Calculate the peak frequency per axis for bursts — peak_frequency
code: a <- acc(   list(     cbind(       X = sin(1:200 / (5  / (pi * 2))),       Z = cos(1:200 / (80 / (pi * 2)))     )   ),    units::set_units(400, "Hz") )  peak_frequency(a) #> [[1]] #> Units: [Hz] #>  X  Z  #> 80  6  #>   peak_frequency(a, units::set_units(.25, "Hz")) #> [[1]] #> Units: [Hz] #>  X  Z  #> 80  5  #>   # Increasing resolution more peak_frequency(a, units::set_units(.005, "Hz")) #> [[1]] #> Units: [Hz] #>      X      Z  #> 79.995  5.115  #>   a <- acc(   list(     cbind(       X = sin((1:200) / (5 / (pi * 2))),       Z = cos(80 + 1:200 / (80 / (pi * 2)))     )   ),    units::set_units(400, "Hz") )  peak_frequency(a, units::set_units(.005, "Hz")) #> [[1]] #> Units: [Hz] #>      X      Z  #> 79.995  4.875  #>

what: Set or convert units on IMU burst data — set_imu_units
title: Set or convert units on IMU burst data — set_imu_units
text: Methods attach convert units burst matrices acc, mag, gyro vectors. method validates target unit dimensionally compatible IMU class: acc: acceleration units (e.g., "m/s^2", "standard_free_fall") mag: magnetic flux density units (e.g., "tesla", "uT", "gauss") gyro: angular velocity units (e.g., "rad/s", "degree/s") calibrate raw accelerometer values rather simply attaching converting units, use acc_calibrate().

title: Set or convert units on IMU burst data — set_imu_units
code: set_imu_units(x, value, ...)

title: Set or convert units on IMU burst data — set_imu_units
text: x acc, mag, gyro vector. value Character specifying target units (e.g., "m/s^2"). units terms gravitational acceleration, use "standard_free_fall". ... Unused.

title: Set or convert units on IMU burst data — set_imu_units
text: input vector units attached burst matrix.

title: Set or convert units on IMU burst data — set_imu_units
code: a <- acc_example()  # Attach units to unitless bursts set_imu_units(a, "m/s^2") #> <acceleration[2]> #> [1] (0.67 0.01 1) [m/s^2]  (0.08 -0.52 1) [m/s^2] #> # frequency: 20 [Hz]  # Convert between units a_ms2 <- set_imu_units(a, "m/s^2") set_imu_units(a_ms2, "standard_free_fall") #> <acceleration[2]> #> [1] (0.07 0 0.1) [standard_free_fall]     (0.01 -0.05 0.1) [standard_free_fall] #> # frequency: 20 [Hz]  # Dimensionally incompatible units error try(set_imu_units(a, "kg")) #> Error in set_imu_units_(x, value, reference = "m/s^2", sensor = "acc") :  #>   kg units not valid for `acc` vector. #> ℹ Units must be convertible to m/s^2

what: Split an IMU vector at regular intervals — split_imu
title: Split an IMU vector at regular intervals — split_imu
text: Split bursts IMU vector bursts given time duration. result list vectors length input, class x.

title: Split an IMU vector at regular intervals — split_imu
code: split_imu(x, interval)

title: Split an IMU vector at regular intervals — split_imu
text: x IMU vector (e.g. acc, mag, gyro). interval Numeric units object defining time intervals x split. units provided, interval assumed period units x (.e., 1 divided frequency units).

title: Split an IMU vector at regular intervals — split_imu
text: list vectors (class x), length x. element contains split pieces corresponding input burst.

title: Split an IMU vector at regular intervals — split_imu
code: a <- acc(   list(cbind(X = 1:60, Y = 1:60), cbind(X = 101:140)),   frequency = c(units::set_units(20, "Hz"), units::set_units(40, "Hz")),   start = as.POSIXct(c(0, 10), tz = "UTC") )  x <- split_imu(a, units::set_units(1, "s")) x #> [[1]] #> <acceleration[3]> #> [1] (10.5 10.5) (30.5 30.5) (50.5 50.5) #> # frequency: 20 [Hz] #> [[2]] #> <acceleration[1]> #> [1] (120.5) #> # frequency: 40 [Hz]  # Flatten to a single vector flat <- purrr::reduce(x, c) flat #> <acceleration[4]> #> [1] (10.5 10.5) (30.5 30.5) (50.5 50.5) (120.5)     #> # frequency: 20 [Hz] - 40 [Hz]  # Start times are updated to match the start of each split component starts(flat) #> [1] "1970-01-01 00:00:00 UTC" "1970-01-01 00:00:01 UTC" #> [3] "1970-01-01 00:00:02 UTC" "1970-01-01 00:00:10 UTC"  # Use merge_imu() on flat identical(merge_imu(flat, drop = TRUE), a) #> [1] TRUE  if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ # In a dataframe, split and unnest to retain index matching library(dplyr) library(tidyr)  tbl <- tibble::tibble(id = c("a", "b"), burst = a)  tbl <- tbl |>   mutate(burst = split_imu(burst, units::set_units(1, "s"))) |>   unnest(burst) |>   mutate(timestamp = starts(burst))  tbl  # Use merge_imu() to recover original bursts tbl |>   mutate(burst = merge_imu(burst, ids = id, drop = FALSE)) } # }

title: move2Acc 0.1.0
text: Initial package development
